User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user of the GlobeFlow application, either a customer seeking immigration assistance or an administrative staff member.

id.description: Unique identifier for the User entity.

fullName.description: The full name of the user.

email.description: The email address of the user, used for login and communication.

role.description: The role of the user within the application, defining their permissions and access level.

language.description: The preferred language for the user interface ('en' for English, 'de' for German).

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the user account was created.

Case.title: Case
Case.description: Represents a single immigration assistance case for a customer, tracking overall progress and status.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Case entity.

userId.description: Reference to the User associated with this immigration case. (Relationship: User 1:N Case)

statusOverall.description: Overall progress of the case, represented as a percentage from 0 to 100.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the immigration case was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating the last time the immigration case was updated.

Document.title: Document
Document.description: Represents a specific document required for the immigration process, including its status and guidance.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Document entity.

caseId.description: Reference to the Case this document belongs to. (Relationship: Case 1:N Document)

type.description: The category or type of the document (e.g., 'Passport copy', 'Proof of address').

title.description: The display title of the document.

whyNeeded.description: Explanation of why this particular document is required for the immigration process.

howToGet.description: Instructions or guidance on how and where to obtain the document in the home country.

status.description: The current status of the document within the review process.

fileUrl.description: URL to the uploaded document file in cloud storage.

reviewerComment.description: Comments or feedback provided by the reviewer if the document is 'rejected' or 'under review'.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating the last time the document's status or details were updated.

Transfer.title: Transfer
Transfer.description: Represents an asset transfer transaction initiated by a customer, tracking its details and progress.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Transfer entity.

caseId.description: Reference to the Case this asset transfer belongs to. (Relationship: Case 1:N Transfer)

homeBankName.description: The name of the user's home bank from which assets are being transferred.

homeBankContactName.description: Name of the contact person at the user's home bank.

homeBankContactInfo.description: Contact information (e.g., email or phone) for the contact person at the home bank.

amount.description: The amount of money being transferred.

currency.description: The currency of the transfer amount (e.g., 'USD', 'EUR').

status.description: The current status of the asset transfer process.

etaDays.description: Estimated number of days until the transfer is completed.

feesEstimated.description: Estimated total fees associated with the asset transfer.

fxRate.description: The foreign exchange rate applied for the transfer, if applicable.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating the last time the transfer's status or details were updated.

Message.title: Message
Message.description: Represents a message in the in-app chat system between a customer and a case manager.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Message entity.

caseId.description: Reference to the Case this message is associated with. (Relationship: Case 1:N Message)

fromUserId.description: Reference to the User who sent this message. (Relationship: User 1:N Message)

fromRole.description: The role of the sender of the message ('customer' or 'admin').

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the message was sent.

Appointment.title: Appointment
Appointment.description: Represents a scheduled appointment between a customer and a case manager.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Appointment entity.

caseId.description: Reference to the Case this appointment is for. (Relationship: Case 1:N Appointment)

slotTime.description: The scheduled date and time for the appointment.

status.description: The current status of the appointment request (e.g., 'requested', 'confirmed', 'cancelled').

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the appointment request was created.

definition.description: Stores user profile data. Each document represents a single user, identified by their authentication UID. Access is restricted to the user themselves or administrators.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user, matching Firebase Authentication UID.

definition.description: A dedicated collection to store the UIDs of users with 'admin' privileges. This enables fast, existence-based authorization checks in security rules. Each document is minimal, typically just the UID as its ID.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the administrator, matching Firebase Authentication UID.

definition.description: Stores individual immigration cases. Each document includes the `userId` field (ownerId) to link to the owning customer, crucial for authorization independence. Accessible by the owning customer and administrators.

params.name: caseId
params.description: The unique identifier for the immigration case.

definition.description: A subcollection storing details for required documents for a specific case. Each document includes the denormalized `userId` (ownerId) from its parent Case for authorization independence. Accessible by the owning customer and administrators.

params.name: caseId
params.description: The unique identifier for the immigration case.

params.name: documentId
params.description: The unique identifier for the document within the case.

definition.description: A subcollection storing asset transfer transaction details for a specific case. Each document includes the denormalized `userId` (ownerId) from its parent Case for authorization independence. Accessible by the owning customer and administrators.

params.name: caseId
params.description: The unique identifier for the immigration case.

params.name: transferId
params.description: The unique identifier for the asset transfer.

definition.description: A subcollection storing in-app chat messages for a specific case. Each document includes the denormalized `userId` (ownerId) from its parent Case for authorization independence. Accessible by the owning customer and administrators.

params.name: caseId
params.description: The unique identifier for the immigration case.

params.name: messageId
params.description: The unique identifier for the message within the case.

definition.description: A subcollection storing scheduled appointments for a specific case. Each document includes the denormalized `userId` (ownerId) from its parent Case for authorization independence. Accessible by the owning customer and administrators.

params.name: caseId
params.description: The unique identifier for the immigration case.

params.name: appointmentId
params.description: The unique identifier for the appointment within the case.

firestore.reasoning:
The recommended Firestore structure prioritizes Authorization Independence and Query Accessibility (QAPs) for both customers and administrators. This is achieved through a combination of top-level collections for primary entities and strategic denormalization of authorization context.

1.  **Authorization Independence via Denormalization:**
    *   All customer-related data (Cases, Documents, Transfers, Messages, Appointments) are stored in top-level collections (`/cases`, `/admin_roles`, etc.) or subcollections (`/cases/{caseId}/documents`).
    *   Critically, the `userId` (representing the owner) is explicitly stored in the `Case` document (`/cases/{caseId}`).
    *   For all subcollections (`documents`, `transfers`, `messages`, `appointments`), the `userId` of the owning customer is denormalized directly into each document. This means a `Document` document at `/cases/{caseId}/documents/{documentId}` will contain its own `userId` field (copied from its parent `Case`).
    *   This strategy eliminates the need for `get()` operations in security rules to check parent documents for ownership. Rules can directly evaluate `request.auth.uid == resource.data.userId` for any document, making them atomic, performant, and significantly easier to debug.

2.  **QAPs (Rules are not Filters):**
    *   **For Customers:** Customers can query for their specific data by using `where('userId', '==', request.auth.uid)` on collections like `/cases` or by directly accessing subcollections like `/cases/{caseId}/documents` where the rule `resource.data.userId == request.auth.uid` ensures only owned data is returned. This guarantees that `list` operations always return only authorized data, respecting Query Access Patterns.
    *   **For Administrators:** The use of top-level collections for `Cases` (and its subcollections) combined with a dedicated `admin_roles` collection allows administrators to perform broad `list` queries (e.g., list all cases) without being restricted by a `userId` segment in the path. Admin access is granted by checking for the existence of `request.auth.uid` in the `/admin_roles` collection, which is highly efficient. This allows rules to be written like `allow read: if request.auth.uid == resource.data.userId || get(/admin_roles/$(request.auth.uid)).exists();`, supporting both customer and admin list operations effectively.

3.  **DBAC (Database Access Control):** A dedicated top-level collection `/admin_roles/{userId}` is used to store the UIDs of administrators. This 'existence over content' approach provides a fast and explicit way to check for administrative privileges directly within security rules, aligning with DBAC principles without relying on custom claims or complex document reads.

4.  **Structural Segregation & Predictability:** The data is segregated into collections with clear, consistent security postures. Path wildcards (`{userId}`, `{caseId}`, `{documentId}`) are semantic and descriptive. Explicit state modeling (e.g., `status` fields) ensures data clarity.
